name: Reserve Bank of India
summary: Day 92 (Saturday May 30; week ending 22 May data). **THE INTERVENTION COST IS MOUNTING — FOREX RESERVES FELL $7.5bn IN A WEEK TO $681.4bn (~$37bn OFF PEAK). The sharp weekly drawdown (RBI weekly statistical supplement, week ending 22 May) is the price of defending the rupee through the Rs96.96 record-low episode (20 May) and into the ~Rs95.0 firming. The RBI's $5bn dollar-rupee buy-sell swap drew ~$9.8bn in bids (healthy bank appetite, NOT stress). India 10Y G-Sec eased to ~7.00% on the ceasefire + lower Brent, capped by an upcoming debt auction + RBI policy. No NEW macroprudential directive this window — the toolkit ($5bn swap + ~$1bn/day spot sales + NDF controls + NOP cap) is unchanged; the ABSENCE of fresh escalation is mildly reassuring. The reserves drawdown is the constraint: continued intervention at this pace is not indefinitely sustainable, which is why an organic FII turn (not a Hormuz deal) is the real rupee-stabilizer.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **MACROPRUDENTIAL ESCALATION — THE RBI IS REACHING DEEP INTO THE REGULATORY TOOLBOX AND THE RUPEE STILL WON'T HOLD ₹95 = A STRESS SIGNAL.** The intervention architecture is now stacked across every register: the $5B buy-sell USD/INR swap auction (May), ~$1B/day spot dollar sales, NDF position controls (April 1 barred AD banks from offering rupee NDF to corporates; partial lift April 20), bank Net Open Position capped ~$100M/day, and ~$700B of reserves deployed. Despite all of it — and despite operative Brent sub-$100 (oil import-bill relief in hand) — USD/INR sits ~₹95.68 and the rupee will not firm. The binding constraint is record FII outflows (~₹3.33 lakh crore FY26, Republic World May 28) + 15-year-low FPI ownership: intervention can CAP the currency but cannot reverse foreign positioning, and when a central bank uses this much of its toolbox and the currency still won't hold ₹95 on an oil-relief tape, that is the stress signal. **Structural picture unchanged**: room to ease from 5.25% remains GONE (cuts accelerate FII exit); >$38B of reserves burned since Feb 28 (from the $728.49B peak); the rupee printed an all-time low ₹96.96 on May 20. The next reserves print (May 30, week ending May 22) captures this week's intervention — cleanest forward sustainability test. PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (~48h window — NO brief May 26). **THE $5B SWAP RESULT: ~1.96x COVER AT A ₹9.10 CUT-OFF — HEALTHY BANK DOLLAR-APPETITE, NOT FUNDING STRESS.** The RBI conducted its $5B three-year USD/INR BUY-SELL FX swap May 26: received 254 bids worth $9.8bn, accepted 141 — a ~1.96x cover ratio — at a ₹9.10 cut-off forward premium (near-leg settle May 29 2026; far-leg maturity May 29 2029; injects rupee liquidity while RBI takes in dollars now / returns them in 3 years). The ~2x cover + orderly cut-off read as constructive against the rupee slide + reserve drawdown — the dollar-funding-stress test the May 25 brief flagged came back BENIGN. This pairs with the May 25 RBI 'rupee undervalued' jawboning = a coordinated liquidity-plus-verbal-support posture (CONFIRMED — Invezz, WION). **But the swap did NOT turn the tape**: the US CONFIRMED self-defense strikes inside southern Iran May 25 'dampened hopes for a peace deal,' the crude rebound reversed the rupee's appreciation, and USD/INR gave back the May 25 firming to ₹95.68 (+0.4%, rupee WEAKER) by May 27. **The structural picture is unchanged**: RBI's room to ease from 5.25% remains GONE (cuts accelerate FII exit); >$38B of reserves burned since Feb 28 (from the $728.49B peak); the rupee printed an all-time low ₹96.96 on May 20 before off-market sales clawed it back; the FOURTH fuel hike (₹2.61-2.71/L May 25, cumulative ~₹7.5/L; no fifth hike this window) compresses optionality via certain core-CPI feed-through. The next reserves print (May 30, week ending May 22) captures this week's intervention — cleanest forward sustainability test. Net read: the swap + jawboning are real liquidity-management tools that CAP the currency and signal healthy bank appetite; they do NOT reverse the structural FII/reserve overhang. PRIOR (May 25): Day 87 (Monday May 25 — India open while US/UK closed). **RBI'S FIRST EXPLICIT VERBAL-INTERVENTION NOTE OF THE CYCLE: RBI COMMENTARY THAT THE INR IS 'UNDERVALUED' HELPED STABILISE THE RUPEE, WHICH FIRMED TO ₹95.22 (-1.0%, RUPEE STRONGER) ON MAY 25 — A NOTABLE SHIFT TOWARD JAWBONING, TIMED ONE DAY BEFORE THE $5B 3-YEAR USD/INR BUY-SELL SWAP AUCTION TUESDAY MAY 26 (10:30-11:30 IST; NEAR-LEG SETTLE MAY 29; FAR-LEG MATURITY MAY 29, 2029; INJECTS ₹42,000-43,000 CR DURABLE LIQUIDITY).** The jawboning + oil-relief (Brent <$100) drove the rupee's first clean STRENGTHENING session of the cycle driven by deal+oil rather than pure spot intervention; India 10Y eased to 7.03% (-6 bps) partly on RBI swap-auction liquidity backstop + oil-inflation relief; India VIX collapsed -7.9%. Tuesday's swap-auction cut-off + subscription level is the market read on dollar-funding stress under the reserves drawdown, and the next reserves print landing May 30 (week ending May 22) will capture this week's ~$5B intervention — cleanest forward sustainability test. **But the structural picture is unchanged**: RBI's room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE (cuts accelerate FII exit), Bank Nifty's +2.3% Monday is a relief-day bounce that does not reverse the cumulative defense MTM cost, and the FOURTH fuel hike (₹2.61-2.71/L May 25, cumulative ~₹7.5/L) compresses RBI optionality further via certain core-CPI feed-through. The 'INR undervalued' line is the cleanest new Indian-macro signal — RBI now layering verbal intervention on top of spot sales + the structural swap-auction architecture. PRIOR (May 24): **MAY 26 $5B 3-YEAR USD/INR BUY-SELL SWAP AUCTION CONFIRMED OPERATIONAL DETAILS: 10:30-11:30 IST; NEAR-LEG SETTLEMENT MAY 29; FAR-LEG MATURITY MAY 29, 2029. INJECTS ₹42,000-43,000 CR DURABLE LIQUIDITY. INDIA'S FOREX RESERVES DROPPED $8.094B TO $688.894B IN WEEK ENDING MAY 15 — DOWN FROM FEB 27 PEAK $728.494B (CUMULATIVE ~$39.6B DRAWDOWN IN 11 WEEKS). IMPORT COVER 11→10 MONTHS. WIONEWS AGGREGATION CITES 'OVER $100B IN SPOT AND FORWARD MARKETS DURING 2025-26' — MATERIALLY HIGHER THAN $38B FIGURE; LIKELY CONFLATES PRE-CRISIS WITH CRISIS-ERA INTERVENTION; BEARS VERIFICATION BEFORE TREATED AS AUTHORITATIVE. RESERVES DROP WEEK ENDING MAY 8 TO WEEK ENDING MAY 15 = $8.094B IS THE CLEANEST SINGLE-WEEK INTERVENTION PRINT OF THE POST-MARCH CYCLE.** Tuesday May 26 swap auction cut-off + subscription level is the market read on dollar-funding stress under reserves drawdown. **The next reserves print landing May 30 (week ending May 22) will capture this week's ~$5B intervention** — cleanest forward sustainability test. **RBI's structural FX-liquidity-management architecture is now operationally explicit**: spot intervention + 3Y swap auction + administrative measures (gold/silver duty, NOP cap, NDF prohibition). PRIOR (May 23): RBI CUMULATIVE DEFENSE $38B+ SINCE FEB 28 (BUSINESSTODAY MAY 21). $5B USD/INR 3-YEAR SWAP AUCTION TUESDAY MAY 26 — REGIME SIGNAL: MOVING FROM PURELY SPOT INTERVENTION TO STRUCTURAL FX-LIQUIDITY-MANAGEMENT ARCHITECTURE. USD/INR ₹95.68 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (-0.5% 1D, RUPEE STRENGTHENING; +1.7% MoM; +5.5% 3M). FOREX RESERVES $688.9B WEEK ENDED MAY 15 (-$8B FROM $696.9B).** Friday's tape extends Thursday's intervention pattern. **Reserves print landing May 30 (week ending May 22) will capture this week's ~$5B intervention** — the cleanest forward sustainability test. **Sustainability now operational, not theoretical**: ~$150B deployable before import cover falls to 2013 levels = ~30 weeks at this pace; in stress accelerations, ~8-12 weeks. **The May 26 swap auction is the regime signal**: subscription level + cut-off price = market read on dollar-funding stress under reserves drawdown. Liquidity injection ~₹42,000-43,000 cr — what a central bank does when it wants to preserve reserves while keeping currency capped. **Bank Nifty 54,055.35 +1.2% 1D / -5.4% 1M / -11.8% 3M = cumulative RBI defense MTM cost** (~₹3,000-4,000 cr Q4 MTM losses on bank NDF books); does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. **India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00%** paused near 7.00% after 4-session advance (-0.03 to -0.07pp WoW est. eased on RBI swap-auction + bond-tax-cut government study). **Third petrol/diesel hike May 23 (cumulative ₹4.74 petrol, ₹4.82 diesel over May 15-19-23) compresses RBI policy optionality further** — core CPI feed-through certain; structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE. **Watch trigger May 30**: 'RBI reserves print below $690B' = deployment-capacity narrative becomes dominant Indian-macro signal. PRIOR (May 22): **RBI ~$5B IN 48HR SPOT INTERVENTION DROVE USD/INR FROM INTRA-WEEK RECORD LOW ₹97.15 TO ₹95.69 CLOSE. RBI ANNOUNCED $5B DOLLAR-RUPEE BUY/SELL SWAP AUCTION FOR MAY 26 — REGIME SIGNAL: SHIFTING FROM PURELY SPOT INTERVENTION TO STRUCTURAL FX-LIQUIDITY-MANAGEMENT ARCHITECTURE. FOREX RESERVES $688.9B WEEK ENDED MAY 15 (-$8B FROM $696.9B).** **RBI sold ~$2-3B Thursday + ~$2-3B Friday through large state-run banks** = ~$5B in 48 hours (Business Standard, Bloomberg estimates) — multiples of May 9-15 weekly $7.79B drawdown rate. **The May 26 swap auction is the regime signal**: RBI is moving from purely spot intervention to structural FX-liquidity-management architecture, which is what a central bank does when it wants to preserve reserves while keeping the currency capped. **Forex reserves print landed Friday (Akashvani News + RBI weekly supplement)**: $688.9B for week ended May 15, DOWN $8B from $696.9B — confirms prior week's intervention; next week's print (landing May 30 for week ended May 22) will capture this week's ~$5B intervention. **Sustainability now operational, not theoretical**: at current intervention pace (~$5B per major weekly episode), RBI has ~$150B deployable before import cover falls to 2013 levels = ~30 weeks of buffer at this pace; in stress accelerations, ~8-12 weeks. **Bank Nifty 54,055.35 +1.2% 1D / -5.4% 1M / -11.8% 3M = cumulative RBI defense cost** (~₹3,000-4,000 cr Q4 MTM losses on bank NDF books); does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. **India 10Y G-Sec 7.07% -0.03pp** eased on RBI swap-auction announcement + government bond-tax-cut study. **The ₹96 trigger remains `active_fired`** from May 18; trajectory now split between intervention-driven retracement and structural pressure. **New trigger added**: 'RBI reserves print May 30 (week ending May 22) shows $5B+ drawdown' — confirms structural intervention rate. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: If Pakistan deal signs + Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII flows turn positive → INR retraces toward ₹93-94 organically (intervention can stop). If Pakistan deal fails + Brent re-spikes toward $115-120 → ₹97-98 path opens despite intervention. **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A6 ₹96 TRIGGER FIRES — USD/INR ₹96.28-96.29 ALL-TIME LOW MAY 18 (Day 6 of 5-7 window).** The May 12-added trigger moves from watching → active_fired. RBI binary now operational: (a) defend ₹96 with sustained dollar sales (accelerates reserves drawdown — May 1 was -$7.79B, May 8 +$6.295B forward-book hedging not spot intervention); (b) capitulate to ₹97-98 next pivot; (c) administrative (May 13 gold/silver duty 6%→15% with IGST >18% effective; weekend silver licensed-import regime; April 10 ₹100M NOP cap; April 1 NDF prohibition; FCNR(B) 3.5% swap window). **Sonal Varma (Nomura) '₹97 inside 30 days' call now operationally validated.** **RBI Weekly Statistical Supplement for week ending May 16 has NOT yet printed through Monday morning IST (lands Monday-Tuesday per standard cadence)**; May 8 print shows reserves rebuilt $6.295B to $696.99B (forward-book hedging signal, not spot intervention). **Bank Nifty 53,036.70 -5.4% 1M / -13.8% 3M = cumulative RBI defense cost** (₹3,000-4,000 cr Q4 MTM losses on bank NDF books). **Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates same day) → India imports ~85% crude → every $1 above $83 baseline adds $1.6-2B/yr to import bill → at $111, incremental CAD drain ~$45-55B annualised vs pre-crisis. FII outflow ₹2 lakh cr YTD CY26 exceeds full-year 2025 by ~₹40k cr.** Reuters: rupee worst-performing Asian currency in 2026. Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% Monday. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: crisis (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII positive — neither imminent. RBI ~$150B deployable before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer, but curve steepening. **PRIOR (May 17): RBI FRIDAY WEEKLY RESERVES PRINT DELAYED FROM MAY 16 — WILL LAND MONDAY-TUESDAY**. Per markets-dossier I (Monday May 18 setups): if RBI weekly print >$10B drawdown, sustainability question moves from theoretical to operational. Markets closed Sunday. **A9 RBI FOREX RESERVES — most recent data is week ending May 8 = $696.99B (+$6.3B w/w recovery from May 1 $690.69B).** **USD/INR ₹95.96 Friday close (4th straight record); ₹96 Varma trigger Day 4 of 5-7 window — intraday breach, no closing breach yet. Monday May 18 close is operative test.** **C10 USD/INR ANALYSIS (markets dossier)**: DXY 99.27 + WTI near $106 on Hormuz strain = oil-import-bill pressure direct mechanism; RBI direct intervention near ₹95.96 is the operational defence. Crisis vs non-crisis: ~85% crisis (oil import bill + FII outflow) / ~15% DXY bid. Reversibility: ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow positive — neither imminent. **RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15 — liquidity-providing measure**. India 10Y G-Sec 7.09% (+5 bps 1D May 15); CCIL monthly avg 6.95% vs 7.04% prior. **Bank Nifty -11.9% 3M = RBI REGULATORY DRAG**: cumulative cost of NOP cap + NDF restriction + FCNR(B) swap window MTM losses. Does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime. **PRIOR (May 16):** A9 RBI FOREX RESERVES — most recent data is week ending May 8 = $696.99B (+$6.3B w/w recovery from May 1 $690.69B)**; May 15-ending print not yet released. Yesterday's brief specification ('anything above $10bn this week moves the sustainability question from theoretical to operational') remains a PENDING TEST. The recovery is a modest reprieve consistent with temporary easing of pressure around the summit, not regime change — Brent +1.6% over the summit period and USD/INR intraday high of ₹95.9825 on May 15 (close to ₹96 trigger) suggest the relief was indeed temporary. **A10 USD/INR — May 15 intraday high ₹95.9825 = $0.018 (~1.75 paise) from breaching the ₹96 Varma trigger (Day 4 of 5-7 session window)** — RBI direct intervention near ₹95.96 is the operational defence; pair did not close at the high (rupee under 'strong selling pressure' per Trading Pedia). **Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors** — if announced, partial relief possible. **RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15** (annual trading targets for 21 bond market makers) — liquidity-providing measure; India 10Y stayed flat-to-easing despite global yield shock. **The Bank Nifty -0.8% 1D / -11.9% 3M = RBI REGULATORY DRAG, NOT broad market weakness**: cumulative cost of NOP cap + NDF restriction + FCNR(B) swap window MTM losses. Does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. PRIOR (May 15): **C7 USD/INR ₹95.90 — FOURTH STRAIGHT RECORD CLOSE**; RBI controlling speed not direction — selling dollars near ₹95.96 intervention threshold; pace materially slowed from May 11-12 collapse. **₹96 trigger at Day 3 of 5-7 window — not breached**. At current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. **RBI WEEKLY RESERVES PRINT DUE ~MAY 16 (FRIDAY) = SUSTAINABILITY TEST**. Last week was -$7.79bn; if this week extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer. **C8 BANK NIFTY -0.2% / -4.1% MoM / -11.4% 3M = RBI REGULATORY DRAG, NOT BROAD MARKET WEAKNESS.** RBI March 27 NOP cap ($100M) on banks' Net Open Position INR + April 1 prohibition on AD banks offering NDF contracts to corporates = **Q4 MTM losses estimated ₹3,000-4,000 cr on $30-40B outstanding bank positions**; banks' FX revenue capacity structurally impaired. **This is the regulatory-stress signal the macro-prudential framework warns about**: Bank Nifty weakness is NOT broad market weakness — it is one sector absorbing the cost of RBI's currency-defense toolkit. The 3M -11.4% is the cumulative cost of central bank's currency-defense operations to date. **Does NOT reverse on crisis resolution.** FCNR(B) swap window opened at fixed 3.5% for 3yr+ deposits; NRI dollar deposit scheme (2013 playbook, $26B then) under active study but no decision yet. **Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors — partial relief signal if announced.** Brent +1.6% to $107.42 (refusing to fall on summit); Indian energy import bill pressure persists. India 10Y G-Sec 7.12% (yield easing as traders awaited Trump-Xi summit outcome). **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 MAY 14 MORNING — RECORD CONTINUES, PACE SLOWED FROM MAY 11-12 COLLAPSE**: May 13 close ₹95.31 (4th straight session fresh closing record); RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone holding. Pace materially slowed. **₹96 trigger at Day 2 of 5-7 window — not breached.** At current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. **RBI WEEKLY RESERVES PRINT DUE ~MAY 16 (FRIDAY)** = critical signal — last week was -$7.79bn; if this week extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer. Brent retraced to $105.68 May 14 from May 13 close $108.05 (single-session $108-110 breach not sustained); relief for India's import bill marginal but real. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (month high — investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; Moody's sovereign cut absorbed). **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. Bullish reversal still requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII positive + INR ₹93. PRIOR (May 13): **USD/INR FRESH CLOSING RECORD ₹95.63 MAY 12 (vs ₹95.54 intraday in yesterday's brief); intraday ₹95.58; ₹95.50 RBI direct-intervention threshold NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, not just intraday. May 13 morning ₹95.68 +0.3% / +5bps — pace SLOWED from May 12's +1.2%. RBI macro-prudential toolkit (NOP cap + NDF restriction) tested and broken.** **Moody's CUT INDIA FY26 GDP 0.8PP TO 6.0% (from 6.8%)** = sovereign-rating channel; absorbed by bond market without further spike (10Y G-Sec 6.96-7.00% est. flat). Reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16; markets watching for >$10bn weekly draw as intervention-sustainability question. **NEXT TEST ₹96 within 5-7 sessions if Brent doesn't retrace** = trigger now Day 1 of 5-7 window. Decision-relevant: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% is GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **RBI'S ₹95.50 DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD CLEANLY BROKEN — INR ₹95.54/USD MAY 12 (intraday). The macro-prudential toolkit (NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction operational since May 5) has been TESTED AND BROKEN.** Trigger 'USD/INR crosses ₹95/USD' RE-BREACHED after May 6 reversal. **RBI FOREX RESERVES -$7.79bn TO $690.69bn WEEK ENDED MAY 1 — sharp drawdown, evidence of active intervention; reserves down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak $728.49bn** (~10.5mo import cover, still safe but visibly draining). The curbs slowed but did not stop the move. Bond market: India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95% — auction-anchored despite crude bid. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing: Brent $105.58 → CAD widening → FII -₹8,437 cr single day (highest since April 24) → ₹2.06 lakh cr YTD (exceeds full-year 2025 by ₹40k cr) → reserves drain → weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure.** Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address (defer gold/foreign travel/fertilizer cut/edible oil restraint) explicitly targets FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr drain) — first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle. RBI now in position where the next test is ₹96 within 5-7 sessions (markets dossier C2). **Decision-relevant signal:** RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% is GONE in current regime — cuts would accelerate FII exit. Bullish reversal requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII flow turn positive + INR retracement to ₹93 handle. None imminent. FY27 GDP forecast cut 6.7%→6.2% by global brokerage — energy shock 'historically large with asymmetric macro risks.'

current.india_10y: ~7.00% (eased on ceasefire + lower Brent; capped by upcoming auction + policy)
current.swap: $5bn dollar-rupee buy-sell swap drew ~$9.8bn bids (healthy bank appetite, not stress)
current.directive: No new macroprudential directive this window; toolkit unchanged ($5bn swap + ~$1bn/day spot + NDF controls + NOP cap).

active_triggers.condition: RBI MPC surprises with rate hike (April 6-8)
RBI winds down OMC special-credit-facility on Branch 3 Brent sub-$103 sustained 2-3 sessions
RBI direct INR intervention at ₹95.50 — NOP/NDF curbs prove insufficient
active_triggers.context: NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction held INR through ₹95.36 record May 5; ₹95.50 threshold UNTESTED. If kinetic restart or FII shock accelerates beyond ₹2.5 lakh crore CY26, threshold becomes the next test.

recent_signals.headline: **INTERVENTION COST MOUNTING — FOREX RESERVES -$7.5bn IN A WEEK TO $681.4bn (~$37bn OFF PEAK).** The week-ending-22-May drawdown is the price of defending the rupee through the Rs96.96 record episode and into the ~Rs95 firming; the $5bn buy-sell swap drew ~$9.8bn bids (healthy appetite, not stress). India 10Y eased to ~7.00% on the ceasefire + lower Brent. No new macroprudential directive this window — the absence of fresh escalation is mildly reassuring; the reserves drawdown is the binding constraint.
sources: RBI weekly statistical supplement ($681.4bn, -$7.5bn); Whalesbook; TradingEconomics India 10Y
recent_signals.verification: Reserves $681.4bn / -$7.5bn = CONFIRMED (official); $5bn swap ~$9.8bn bids = CONFIRMED; India 10Y ~7.00% = CONFIRMED (web). No new directive = non-event.

When a central bank uses this much of its toolbox and the currency still won't hold ₹95 on an oil-relief tape, that is the stress signal — not a sign of control. **Structural picture unchanged**: room to ease from 5.25% remains GONE (cuts accelerate FII exit); >$38B of reserves burned since Feb 28 (from the $728.49B peak); the rupee printed an all-time low ₹96.96 on May 20. The next reserves print (May 30, week ending May 22) captures this week's intervention — the cleanest forward sustainability test.

recent_signals.verification: RBI macroprudential escalation — $5B buy-sell swap auction (May), ~$1B/day spot dollar sales, NDF position controls (Apr 1 barred AD-bank corporate rupee NDF / partial lift Apr 20), bank NOP cap ~$100M/day, ~$700B reserves deployed = CONFIRMED ACTIONS (markets dossier C5 + carried policy record); rupee-won't-hold-₹95-despite-deep-toolbox-and-sub-$100-oil = stress signal = CONFIRMED analytical read; USD/INR ~₹95.68 + >$38B reserve drawdown since Feb 28 + ₹96.96 all-time low May 20 = CONFIRMED market/structural data; FY26 FII outflows ~₹3.33 lakh cr = REPORTED (Republic World May 28). No trigger moved to active — the ₹95.50 direct-intervention and ₹96 triggers retain their prior status; no new confirmed-action trigger fired today.

recent_signals.headline: **$5B USD/INR SWAP RESULT — ~1.96x COVER AT ₹9.10 CUT-OFF: HEALTHY DOLLAR APPETITE, NOT FUNDING STRESS; THE DOLLAR-STRESS TEST CAME BACK BENIGN.** The RBI conducted its $5B three-year USD/INR BUY-SELL FX swap on May 26 (the auction the May 25 brief flagged as the dollar-funding-stress read). It received 254 bids worth $9.8bn and accepted 141 — a cover ratio of ~1.96x — at a cut-off forward premium of ₹9.10; near-leg (spot) settles May 29 2026, far-leg matures May 29 2029. The swap injects rupee liquidity into the banking system while the RBI takes in dollars now and returns them in three years. **The mechanism / decision-relevant read**: the ~2x cover and orderly cut-off signal HEALTHY bank appetite for dollar liquidity — NOT acute dollar-funding stress — a constructive signal against the backdrop of the rupee slide and the >$38B reserve drawdown since Feb 28. It pairs with the May 25 'rupee undervalued' jawboning as a coordinated liquidity-plus-verbal-support posture: low-cost, reserve-preserving tools stacked on the structural swap architecture. **But it did NOT turn the tape**: the US CONFIRMED strikes inside southern Iran May 25 'dampened hopes for a peace deal,' the crude rebound reversed the rupee's appreciation, and USD/INR gave back the May 25 firming to ₹95.68 (+0.4%, rupee WEAKER) by May 27. **Structural picture unchanged**: room to ease from 5.25% remains GONE (cuts accelerate FII exit); the all-time-low ₹96.96 printed May 20 (clawed back by off-market sales); the fourth fuel hike (cumulative ~₹7.5/L; no fifth hike this window) compresses optionality via certain core-CPI feed-through. The next reserves print May 30 (week ending May 22) captures this week's intervention — cleanest forward sustainability test. The swap + jawboning CAP the currency and read benign on bank dollar appetite; they do NOT reverse the structural FII/reserve overhang.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-27 A7 (RBI swap result: 254 bids/$9.8bn, 141 accepted, ~1.96x cover, ₹9.10 cut-off)
Markets dossier 2026-05-27 C2 (constructive counter-vector — swap healthy appetite not stress), F (RBI reserves ~$688.9B / -$38B since Feb 28)
Invezz 'Indian Central Bank's $5 billion FX swap draws strong demand' May 26; WION 'RBI announces $5 billion USD/INR swap auction'; Investment Guru India (auction mechanics/dates)
recent_signals.verification: $5B 3Y USD/INR buy-sell swap, 254 bids worth $9.8bn, 141 accepted, ~1.96x cover, ₹9.10 cut-off forward premium, near-leg May 29 2026 / far-leg May 29 2029 = CONFIRMED ACTION (Invezz + WION + Investment Guru India); healthy-dollar-appetite-not-stress framing = CONFIRMED analytical read (markets dossier C2); USD/INR gave back to ₹95.68 + >$38B reserve drawdown = CONFIRMED market/structural data; coordinated-with-May-25-jawboning posture = CONFIRMED analytical observation.

recent_signals.headline: **RBI 'INR UNDERVALUED' — FIRST EXPLICIT VERBAL-INTERVENTION NOTE OF THE CYCLE; RUPEE FIRMED TO ₹95.22 (-1.0%) ONE DAY BEFORE THE $5B SWAP AUCTION.** On May 25, with India open while US/UK were shut for holiday, rupee depreciation 'appears to have stabilised, aided by RBI commentary highlighting that the INR is undervalued' (Business Standard) — the first explicit RBI verbal-intervention/jawboning signal of this kind in the cycle. USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22 (-1.0%, rupee STRONGER), India 10Y eased to 7.03% (-6 bps), and India VIX collapsed -7.9% to ~16.5. **The mechanism**: deal-relief (US-Iran de-escalation hopes) + Brent <$100 (oil import-bill/CAD relief) + the verbal signal combined to produce the first clean rupee-STRENGTHENING session of the cycle driven by oil+deal rather than pure spot intervention — and it lands precisely the day before the **$5B 3-year USD/INR buy-sell swap auction Tuesday May 26 (10:30-11:30 IST; near-leg settle May 29; far-leg maturity May 29, 2029; injects ₹42,000-43,000 cr durable liquidity)**. Jawboning is a low-cost reserve-preserving tool stacked on top of the spot intervention + structural swap architecture. **Regime read**: Tuesday's swap-auction cut-off + subscription = the market read on dollar-funding stress under the reserves drawdown; the next reserves print May 30 (week ending May 22) will capture this week's ~$5B intervention. **Structural picture unchanged**: room to ease from 5.25% remains GONE (cuts accelerate FII exit); the FOURTH fuel hike (₹2.61-2.71/L May 25, cumulative ~₹7.5/L) compresses optionality further via certain core-CPI feed-through; Bank Nifty +2.3% Monday is a relief-day bounce, not a reversal of the cumulative defense MTM cost. The verbal intervention is real and notable, but it caps the currency — it does not reverse the structural FII/reserve overhang.
sources: Business Standard May 25 (RBI 'INR undervalued' commentary; USD/INR ₹95.22; rupee stabilisation read)
Markets dossier 2026-05-25 Section A + C2 + D (swap auction details, India 10Y 7.03%)
recent_signals.verification: RBI 'INR undervalued' verbal-intervention note = CONFIRMED (Business Standard); USD/INR ₹95.22 (-1.0%) + India 10Y 7.03% (-6 bps) + VIX -7.9% = CONFIRMED market data (markets dossier Section A live India prints); $5B swap-auction operational details (May 26, 10:30-11:30 IST) = CONFIRMED (markets dossier D); first-verbal-intervention-of-cycle framing = CONFIRMED analytical read.

recent_signals.headline: **RBI DEFENSE POSTURE: NO HARD INTERVENTION OF ₹96 DURING POSTPONEMENT WINDOW — RUPEE WEAKENED TO ₹96.44 FRESH RECORD LOW DESPITE TRUMP POSTPONEMENT.** RBI may be choosing NOT to defend ₹96 hard to preserve dry powder for a potential kinetic event within the May 24 NYT-window or after. Reserves drawdown sequence: $7.79B drawdown to $690.69B (week ending May 1), rebuilt $6.295B to ~$696.99B (week ending May 8); next print pending Friday May 22. From Feb peak $728.49B, cumulative drawdown is ~$30B+. Business Standard frames reserves as 'still robust to defend rupee' with $150B deployable before 2013 import-cover trough — but the choice to deploy compresses rather than absolves the structural problem. **The fuel pass-through firing (₹0.90 petrol/diesel hike, second in a week) is the operational signal that fiscal capacity has met its limit before FX capacity** — RBI's hands are not yet tied, but the political economy of the OMC under-recovery has reached the wall.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A4, A13; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: RBI reserves print = CONFIRMED operational; no hard defense of ₹96 = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (rupee at fresh low); fuel-pass-through firing = CONFIRMED action (WION + Goodreturns).

recent_signals.headline: RBI ₹95.50 DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD CLEANLY BROKEN — INR ₹95.54 May 12 intraday record; toolkit (NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction) TESTED AND BROKEN; RBI FOREX RESERVES -$7.79bn TO $690.69bn WEEK ENDED MAY 1 (down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak $728.49bn = active intervention not stopping); India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99% (new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95%, auction-anchored despite crude bid); FII -₹8,437.56 cr single-day May 11 (highest since April 24); YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr (exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr); DII absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr). Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address explicitly targets FX pressure (defer gold purchases 1 year + foreign travel 1 year + cut fertilizer 50%). FY27 GDP forecast cut 6.7%→6.2% by global brokerage. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII positive + INR ₹93. Next test ₹96 within 5-7 sessions.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs (primary)
